subject: Sports
title: Canadiens vs Bruins: Hockey's Original Hatred
description: A short lesson to help you apply Canadiens vs Bruins: Hockey's Original Hatred.

hook.text: French Canada vs English America. The Montreal Canadiens and Boston Bruins rivalry is hockey's oldest fire.

buildup.text: They first played in 1924. The Canadiens represent French-Canadian identity; the Bruins embody tough American hockey.

discovery.text: The two teams have met in 34 playoff series—more than any other NHL pairing—with Montreal leading 25-9.

twist.text: The 1979 'Too Many Men on the Ice' penalty against Boston led to a Canadiens goal and series win—still debated.

climax.text: Even as both teams' fortunes have fluctuated, the hatred between their fan bases remains as fierce as ever.

punchline.text: French pride vs Boston grit. A century of hockey hatred.

quiz.question: What cultural divide fuels the Canadiens vs Bruins rivalry?
options: Both teams compete for the same local fans
